steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to specify the location of Ctesiphon. The notes contain exactly this information: Ctesiphon was the empire’s capital and was located near present-day Baghdad, Iraq. A correct answer must state where Ctesiphon is, not just that it existed or was a capital.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘The Sasanian Empire began in AD 224 and ended in AD 651.’: This states dates for the empire — it says nothing about the location of Ctesiphon. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘The capital of the Sasanian Empire, which spanned 1.4 million square miles, was Ctesiphon.’: This names Ctesiphon as the capital and notes the empire’s size, but it does not say where Ctesiphon was located. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘The Sasanians controlled an area of 1.4 million square miles, including present-day Iran and Iraq.’: This describes the empire’s territory but does not mention Ctesiphon’s location at all. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Ctesiphon, the capital of the Sasanian Empire, was located near present-day Baghdad, Iraq.’: This names Ctesiphon and directly specifies its geographic location (near present-day Baghdad, Iraq), exactly what the notes provide and the student needs. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
